title: PostgreSQL for internal tools | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: PostgreSQL for internal tools | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: Why PostgreSQL is often a strong choice for internal systems, workflow applications, and business software with structured relational data.

page.title: PostgreSQL for internal tools | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: PostgreSQL for internal tools and business web applications
page.description: Why PostgreSQL is often a strong choice for internal systems, workflow applications, and business software with structured relational data.

hero.title: The database is an operational foundation, not a footnote
hero.subtitle: In internal systems and workflow-heavy apps, reliability, relational structure, and room for data growth matter more than trend-driven database choices.

intro: PostgreSQL is often a strong choice where applications rely on relationships, state history, structured querying, and long-term data model growth.
Its value is not only performance. It is also about predictability, strong querying, and a cleaner basis for application logic.

sections.title: Where this technology is a strong fit
body: Technology should fit the product and delivery reality, not just developer preference.
bullets: long-term growth of an internal system

sections.title: What to be careful about
body: A technology’s strengths disappear quickly when it is used in the wrong context or without a sound architecture.

sections.title: Typical fit for a business project
body: The choice should support delivery speed, maintainability, and operational confidence over time.
bullets: admin interfaces with real data logic
portals with history and relational data

faq.question: Is PostgreSQL suitable for smaller applications too?
faq.answer: Yes, especially if they work with relational data and may need to grow in complexity over time.

faq.question: Does the database solve performance by itself?
faq.answer: No. Data model, queries, caching, and application design still matter.

faq.question: Does it make sense without a full BI setup?
faq.answer: Yes. Much of the value is in having stronger operational data structure in the product itself.

for: admin interfaces with real data logic
portals with history and relational data
